Remote images are used with next/image but no Next config is shown to allow external domains. In `components/cart-drawer.tsx` and `app/page.tsx`, Image `src` points to `https://images.unsplash.com/...`. Without `images.remotePatterns` / `images.domains` in `next.config.*`, this causes runtime/build error: unconfigured host for next/image.

Likely missing required UI component files/imports. Multiple files import from `@/components/ui/*` (`button`, `badge`, `sheet`, `card`, `tabs`, `input`, `label`, `radio-group`) and `@/lib/utils`, but these files are not included in provided snippets. If absent in project, build fails with module-not-found errors (notably in `components/site-header.tsx`, `components/cart-drawer.tsx`, `app/page.tsx`, `app/shop/page.tsx`, `app/cart/page.tsx`, `app/checkout/page.tsx`).
`app/layout.tsx` imports `ReactNode` type but does not use it (`children` is typed as `React.ReactNode`). This is dead import/noise.
In `app/shop/page.tsx`, Tabs UI is present but not wired to filtering; all products render regardless of selected tab. This is misleading UX (controls imply behavior that doesn’t happen).

Some icon-only action buttons lack explicit accessible names. In `components/cart-drawer.tsx` and `app/cart/page.tsx`, quantity +/- and remove buttons rely on icons only; add aria-labels for screen reader clarity.

`components/site-header.tsx` includes nav link to `/checkout` and `/cart` as primary top-level destinations, which is unusual IA and can reduce conversion clarity; typically checkout is contextual from cart state.
SEO metadata is minimal (only title/description in `app/layout.tsx`). Missing richer metadata (open graph, twitter, canonical, alternates) is non-blocking but limits share/search quality.

`app/categories/page.tsx` links to `/category/${category.slug}`, which implies a dynamic route at `app/category/[slug]/page.tsx`. The provided file is `app/category/slug/page.tsx` (static `slug` segment), so `/category/hela-bonor` etc. will 404 at runtime.

In `app/category/slug/page.tsx`, the page component types `params` as `Promise<{ slug: string }>` and awaits it. In Next.js App Router, `params` is normally a plain object (`{ slug: string }`) for route components. This may not always break immediately but is a fragile/non-idiomatic typing pattern.

`app/page.tsx` uses external Unsplash URLs in `<Image />`. If `next.config` does not allow `images.unsplash.com` via `images.remotePatterns`/`images.domains`, image optimization will fail at runtime/build.

`app/page.tsx` links to `/shop` and `/products`, and product/category cards link to `/product/${id}`. If corresponding route files are not present, these links will lead to 404 pages. (Not confirmed from provided snippets, but high risk.)

problem: `components/login-form.tsx` declares a local `type FormState` while also importing `FormState` from `@/components/form-state`, creating an identifier conflict (`Import declaration conflicts with local declaration of 'FormState'`). This is a build-blocking TypeScript error.

`components/login-form.tsx` imports `HTMLFormElement` from `@/components/html-form-element` and then uses it in `React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>`. This shadows the global DOM `HTMLFormElement` type and will fail unless that module exists and exports a compatible type/value. Given the path, this is very likely a broken import and type error.
`package.json` only lists `recharts` and `lucide-react`, but the codebase uses `next`, `react`, and `react-dom` across app router files. Without those dependencies declared, install/build will fail.

Multiple files import `@/components/ui/*` and `@/lib/utils` (e.g., `components/site-header.tsx`, `app/page.tsx`, `components/login-form.tsx`), but those files are not shown in provided snippets. If not present in project output, this causes module resolution/build failure.

`app/layout.tsx` imports `ReactNode` but does not use it; `components/login-form.tsx` has several extraneous/duplicate imports and spacing artifacts. Not blocking, but reduces maintainability and can fail stricter lint configs.

Pages use remote Unsplash URLs with `next/image` (e.g., `app/page.tsx`, `app/platform/page.tsx`, `app/security/page.tsx`). If `next.config` does not whitelist `images.unsplash.com` via `images.remotePatterns/domains`, runtime/image optimization errors will occur.
`app/robots.ts` explicitly allows `/login` indexing. For auth-like routes, indexing is often undesirable; consider revisiting crawl policy for privacy/security UX.
`app/layout.tsx` sets `lang="sv"` and `openGraph.locale="sv_SE"`, but substantial UI/metadata copy is English. This mismatch may weaken i18n clarity and SEO targeting.
